title: Transportation Passes * Older Adults - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Transportation Passes * Older Adults 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Older Adults AIDS/HIV Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO SENIOR SERVICES CENTER SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home delivered meals, naturalization assistance, homebound assistance, limited transportation assistance, case management, health and wellness activities, and focal point leadership for older adults and their caregivers. An Information and Assistance specialist is available to provide information and referrals. 10165 SE Cherry Blossom Drive Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Some services have income eligibility criteria. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Meals: Varies (503) 484-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW SOUTHEAST MULTICULTURAL SENIOR CENTER TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists older adults and adults with disabilities in accessing transportation. Provides escorted door-to-door rides, monthly TriMet bus and LIFT tickets for those who qualify, and group rides to major grocery stores, food banks, and recreational outings. 4610 SE Belmont Street Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 226-0700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION SENIOR SERVICES IMPACT NW TRANSPORTATION SERVICES
